description_long: This mosque complex features a collection of sand-colored rectangular buildings with flat roofs, some with arched entrances and small decorative elements. The structures are arranged in a courtyard-like layout, with palm trees and small human figures suggesting scale and context. The main building is the tallest, flanked by smaller auxiliary structures, and there are no visible domes or minarets. The surfaces are weathered, giving a sense of age and desert environment, and some buildings have small awnings or canopies in muted colors. The presence of palm trees and the architectural simplicity evoke a North African or Middle Eastern desert town. No intricate geometric patterns or Arabic calligraphy are visible on the exterior surfaces.
description: A sand-colored mosque complex with flat-roofed rectangular buildings, arched entrances, and palm trees, evoking a desert environment. The layout is simple, with weathered surfaces and no visible domes or minarets.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main entrance, palm trees, small human figures, and the arrangement of buildings in a courtyard layout.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the flat roofs, arched entrances, and the relative heights of the main and auxiliary buildings.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the backs of the buildings, palm trees in the background, and the simple, unadorned surfaces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the arrangement of the buildings, additional palm trees, and the open courtyard space.
attribute_architectural_style: North African or Middle Eastern vernacular, characterized by flat roofs, simple rectangular forms, and minimal ornamentation.
